outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Day 31: Trump tries to conjure new reality as Iran war hits one-month fork in road; 4 IDF soldiers killed in Lebanon
outlets.summary: CNN analysis examines Trump's contradictory messaging as war hits 1-month mark: simultaneously claiming 'regime change achieved,' threatening to obliterate infrastructure, and signaling willingness to end without reopening Hormuz.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump declares 'regime change achieved' in Iran — experts warn it's premature
outlets.summary: WaPo scrutinizes Trump's claim in an FT interview that 'regime change has been achieved' in Iran, quoting Iran policy experts who warn the regime remains intact and the claim could harden Iranian resolve against negotiation.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Day 31: IDF strikes 120+ targets in Tehran; Haifa oil refinery ablaze as war escalates
outlets.summary: NPR covers the largest single-night Israeli air campaign of the war alongside the Haifa Bazan refinery fire, noting the parallel energy infrastructure targeting: Israel hit Tabriz Petrochemical, Iran hit Haifa Bazan on the same day.

outlets.name: New York Times
outlets.headline: Knesset approves record $45.8B defense budget as Netanyahu avoids elections; IMF warns of 'largest oil disruption in history'
outlets.summary: NYT leads with Israel's record defense budget and its political implications, alongside the IMF's strongest warning yet about the war's economic consequences.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: 82nd Airborne arrives in Middle East as Trump sets April 6 ultimatum; watchdog: media 'war crime' label 88% directed at US-Israel
outlets.summary: Fox leads with US military buildup and Trump's tough stance, alongside CAMERA media watchdog report that BBC/CNN/NYT used 'war crime' label almost exclusively against US-Israel.

outlets.name: NY Post
outlets.headline: Trump: 'Regime change achieved' in Iran — threatens to take $1 trillion oil island
outlets.summary: NY Post leads with Trump's most aggressive statement yet — claiming regime change and threatening to seize Kharg Island — framing it as maximum pressure working.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Spain's airspace snub exposes European weakness as Trump wages war on Islamism
outlets.summary: Conservative outlet covers Spain's airspace closure as a failure of European resolve, backing Trump's trade tariff threat against Madrid and calling out the Sanchez government's 'appeasement' of Iran.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Spain bans US military flights: Trump vows tariffs against NATO ally that chose Iran's side
outlets.summary: Breitbart frames Spain's decision as a betrayal of the Western alliance, cheering Trump's tariff threat and suggesting other NATO members who oppose the war should face similar consequences.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Kuwaiti oil tanker hit in Dubai port; 3 UN peacekeepers killed; Saudi intercepts 5 missiles targeting oil infrastructure
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with the Al-Salmi tanker attack at Dubai port, the UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in Lebanon, and Saudi Arabia's interception of Iranian missiles targeting its Eastern Province oil facilities.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Iran war: IMF warns of 'largest oil disruption in history' as Pope Leo condemns those who 'wage war'
outlets.summary: BBC frames Day 31 around the IMF's economic warning and the Pope's moral condemnation, balancing the military and humanitarian dimensions.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Israel bombs Tabriz Petrochemical; Iran fires on Haifa refinery and kills Indian worker in Kuwait
outlets.summary: Reuters covers symmetric energy infrastructure strikes: IDF hit Tabriz Petrochemical, Iranian forces fired on Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery. Also reports Indian worker death at Kuwait civilian plant and Trump's 'regime change achieved' claim.

outlets.name: AP
outlets.headline: Trump claims regime change, threatens oil island seizure; Spain defies US as war enters Day 31
outlets.summary: AP covers Trump's escalating rhetoric claiming 'regime change achieved' and Kharg Island seizure threat alongside Spain's historic airspace ban — the first NATO defiance over the Iran war. Indian worker killed in Kuwait adds humanitarian dimension.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Netanyahu: War 'beyond halfway point'; Knesset passes record $45.8B budget; IDF says close to finishing Iran arms industry
outlets.summary: Times of Israel covers Netanyahu's Newsmax claims of war progress, the politically crucial budget vote, and the IDF's assessment of Iran's remaining military capacity.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Israeli support for Iran war wanes as opposition more than doubles; poll shows 11.5% of Israeli Jews now oppose the war
outlets.summary: Haaretz leads with polling data showing growing Israeli opposition to the war, with Jewish opposition more than doubling from 4% to 11.5% over four weeks.

outlets.name: Ynet
outlets.headline: Massive fire at Bazan oil refinery in Haifa after Iranian missile hits; 120+ IDF strikes in Tehran
outlets.summary: Ynet covers the Haifa Bazan refinery fire extensively, noting it is the first confirmed hit on a major Israeli energy processing facility. Also covers the IDF Alpinist Unit raid and Spain's airspace closure creating diplomatic complications for Israel.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Resistance forces strike Kuwaiti tanker at Dubai port; Mojtaba Khamenei mourns navy chief Tangsiri as 'guardian of Islam'
outlets.summary: Iranian state media frames the Al-Salmi tanker attack as legitimate resistance and leads with Mojtaba Khamenei's condolence for IRGC Navy Chief Tangsiri.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Iran forces strike Haifa oil refinery; IDF criminally targets Tabriz Petrochemical in civilian attack
outlets.summary: IRNA frames Day 31 as 'symmetric retaliation': Iran's resistance forces hit the Haifa Bazan refinery while condemning IDF strikes on Tabriz Petrochemical as an attack on civilian industrial infrastructure. Spain airspace closure framed as global isolation of US-Israeli aggression.

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: IRGC confirms martyrdom of Navy Chief Tangsiri; Pezeshkian warns of economic strain; NATO's missile claim rejected
outlets.summary: Tasnim covers Tangsiri's martyrdom, reports on economic challenges while rejecting NATO's claim of intercepting Iranian missile over Turkey.
